input_row.title: Minimize Container Registries to only those approved
input_row.rationale: Allowing unrestricted access to external container registries provides the opportunity for malicious or unapproved containers to be deployed into the cluster. Allowlisting only approved container registries reduces this risk. Impact: All container images to be deployed to the cluster must be hosted within an approved container image registry.
input_row.remediation: If you are using Azure Container Registry you have this option: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/container-registry/container-registry-firewall- access-rules For other non-AKS repos using admission controllers or Azure Policy will also work. Limiting or locking down egress traffic is also recommended: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/limit-egress-traffic References: 1. https://docs.microsoft.com/security/benchmark/azure/security-controls-v2-asset- management#am-6-use-only-approved-applications-in-compute-resources 2. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/aks/limit-egress-traffic 3. https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/container-registry/container-registry- firewall-access-rules
input_row.impact: All container images to be deployed to the cluster must be hosted within an approved container image registry.

gpt_response:
**AUDIT APPROACH:** Automated

**APPROACH DECISION:** 
- The control’s audit field is empty, but the rationale indicates the need to restrict container registries. This can be verified through Azure APIs.
- The remediation section specifies using Azure Container Registry and mentions leveraging Azure Policy, which indicates the use of programmable interfaces.
- The control does not require SSH or file system access and can be evaluated through API queries regarding container images and registries.

**PROGRAM NAME:** azure_aks_container_registries_only_approved

**AUTOMATION DETAILS:**
- The program will check the configuration of Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) clusters to ensure that only images from approved Azure Container Registries are utilized.
- It will utilize Azure Resource Manager APIs to enumerate AKS clusters and their configurations.
- The specific checks will include:
  1. Querying the AKS cluster configuration to identify the allowed container registries.
     - Example API endpoint: `GET https://management.azure.com/subscriptions/{subscriptionId}/resourceGroups/{resourceGroupName}/providers/Microsoft.ContainerService/managedClusters/{resourceName}?api-version=2021-08-01`
  2. Validating that the `containerRegistry` property lists only approved registry URLs.
  3. If admission controllers are used, verifying that Azure Policy is in place to enforce this restriction.

**Pass/Fail Criteria:**
- **Pass:** If the AKS cluster configuration lists only approved container registries and restricts external access.
- **Fail:** If any unauthorized container registries are listed or if access is unrestricted.

The automated check will ensure compliance with the best practices outlined in the CIS Benchmark for Azure Kubernetes Service.
